note: All display rooms are barrier-free via ramps/lift with no steps; free loaner wheelchairs (indoor & park), accessible toilets, free companion entry, direct garden ramp. The hilltop Gloriette is a challenging climb. A must-see.
Barrier-free with ramps and lifts (one entrance uses a neat rising-platform to door height); Imperial Apartments, Silver Collection and Sisi Museum all accessible. Plan time - it's huge.
Accessible via a side entrance on the north side; main floor and altar area reachable. The towers are NOT accessible (steep narrow stairs). Stephansplatz outside is flat.
Fully accessible with ramps and lifts to the exhibitions (world's largest Klimt collection, 'The Kiss'); accessible toilets and parking. Baroque gardens largely navigable.
Ramps and accessible pathways through the park; the iconic Wiener Riesenrad Ferris wheel is wheelchair accessible. Some individual rides vary - check ahead. Fun for families.
Step-free entrances, lifts and ramps throughout the grand art-history halls; tactile tours for visual impairment. Fully committed to access.
Central park with flat paths all the way round and the gilded Johann Strauss monument; easy, relaxed, stroller-friendly.
Vienna's big open-air market; flat and largely navigable, though busy stalls get tight. Great food stop.
Accessible entrance with a wheelchair ramp and dedicated accessible seats (book early - they sell out). Grand and worth a performance or tour.
The historic core is relatively accessible - Graben and Kohlmarkt have been re-laid with flat well-fitted cobbles; some side streets stay rougher. Pleasant, walkable centre.
